Exceptions.Message: Response status code does not indicate success: Forbidden (Forbidden).

Findings.Title: Block legacy authentication protocols
Findings.Detail: Legacy authentication blocked by 1 Conditional Access policy(ies).
Findings.Remediation: Create a Conditional Access policy: All users, All cloud apps, Client apps = Other clients, Grant = Block. Roll out in report-only first.

Findings.Title: Phishing-resistant MFA enforced for privileged roles
Findings.Detail: No Conditional Access policy enforces a phishing-resistant Authentication Strength on privileged roles.
Findings.RequiredValue: FIDO2 / WHfB / Cert-based for all admin roles
Findings.Remediation: Create a Conditional Access policy targeting all privileged role members requiring FIDO2 or certificate-based authentication via Authentication Strengths.

Findings.Title: MFA registered for all enabled user accounts
Findings.Detail: MFA registration critically low at 80%. Enforcing AAD-3.1 CA MFA policy at this registration level will lock out 1 users.
Findings.Remediation: Enable Registration Campaign (Entra ID > Authentication methods > Registration campaign). Use Temporary Access Pass (TAP) for bulk onboarding without helpdesk calls. Do not enforce AAD-3.1 until registration exceeds 95%.

Findings.Title: MFA enforced via Conditional Access (not per-user MFA or Security Defaults)
Findings.CurrentValue: Security Defaults: disabled — tenant managed via Conditional Access
Findings.RequiredValue: Security Defaults: disabled; MFA enforcement via Conditional Access

Findings.Title: Security Defaults disabled in favor of Conditional Access
Conditional Access: Legacy authentication blocked
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy blocking client app types: other, exchangeActiveSync

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: MFA required for all users on all cloud apps
Findings.Detail: No enabled CA policy requires MFA for All users on All cloud apps. Compromised credentials provide immediate account access.
Findings.CurrentValue: No MFA CA policy for all users
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy: All users (exclude break-glass), All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA
Findings.Remediation: Create CA policy: All users (exclude break-glass named location), All cloud apps, Grant = Require MFA. Deploy report-only first. Do not enforce until AAD-2.1 registration exceeds 95%.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: MFA required for Azure management
Findings.Detail: No enabled CA policy enforces MFA for Azure management (Azure portal, CLI, PowerShell). App ID: 797f4846-ba00-4fd7-ba43-dac1f8f63013.
Findings.CurrentValue: No MFA CA policy for Azure management
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting Microsoft Azure Management app with MFA grant
Findings.Remediation: Add Microsoft Azure Management to existing all-users MFA CA policy, or create a dedicated policy. Covers Azure portal, Azure CLI, and Azure PowerShell.

Findings.Title: No Conditional Access policies remaining in report-only mode
Findings.Detail: Report-only policies provide zero enforcement. They generate logs but do not block or require anything.
Findings.CurrentValue: 9 report-only policy/policies: [SharePoint admin center]Block access from apps on unmanaged devices - 2026/01/15; Require multifactor authentication for all users; Require MDM-enrolled and compliant device to access cloud apps for all users (Preview); Require phishing-resistant multifactor authentication for admins; Geo-Blocking; Require Device authentication joining devices; Insider Risk Policy - Block; Insider Risk - MFA; Insider Risk - Allow
Findings.RequiredValue: All security CA policies in enabled (enforced) state
Findings.Remediation: Review each. Enforce or document acceptance with rationale and target date. Report-only provides zero protection.

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: Sign-in risk policy configured (Entra ID P2)
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy: 'Sign in risk - Block' [Risk levels: high]
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting sign-in risk Medium/High with MFA or block response

Findings.Title: Conditional Access: User risk policy configured (Entra ID P2)
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled CA policy targeting user risk High with block or password change response

Findings.Title: Authenticator app number matching enabled
Findings.CurrentValue: Number matching: enabled (Microsoft default or explicit). Additional context: default

Findings.Title: Global Administrator count between 2 and 5
Findings.RequiredValue: Between 2 and 5 permanent Global Administrator assignments

Findings.Title: No on-premises synced accounts hold Entra ID admin roles
Findings.CurrentValue: No on-premises synced accounts found with Entra ID role assignments
Findings.RequiredValue: Zero on-premises synced accounts in any Entra ID directory role

Findings.Title: Global Administrator accounts are dedicated (no assigned licenses)
Findings.Detail: Licensed GA accounts are daily-use accounts — exposed to phishing, malware, and browser-based attacks during normal work. A compromised daily-use account with GA is an immediate full-tenant compromise.
Findings.CurrentValue: GA accounts with assigned licenses (likely daily-use): mlevorson@nextlayersec.io
Findings.RequiredValue: All GA accounts unlicensed — admin tasks use dedicated cloud-only account, daily work uses separate licensed account
Findings.Remediation: Create dedicated cloud-only admin accounts: unlicensed, no mailbox, used only for admin tasks. Remove GA role from daily-use licensed accounts. Assign daily-use accounts appropriate scoped roles only.

Findings.Title: PIM used for Global Administrator role (JIT access model)
Findings.Detail: PIM is available but not consistently applied to the Global Administrator role. Permanent standing access means a compromised admin account immediately has full tenant privileges.
Findings.RequiredValue: All operational GA assignments via PIM eligible; zero or break-glass-only permanent assignments
Findings.Remediation: Migrate all operational GA assignments to PIM eligible. Activation settings: require MFA, justification required, max 8-hour duration. Consider approval workflow for production tenants.

Findings.Title: Break-glass Global Administrator account exists
Findings.Detail: Break-glass candidate(s) detected based on heuristic (unlicensed, cloud-only GA, sign-in > 30 days or never). Manual verification required — credential storage, CA exclusion, and sign-in alert cannot be confirmed automatically.
Findings.RequiredValue: Verified break-glass GA: cloud-only, unlicensed, credentials sealed offline, sign-in alert configured, tested quarterly
Findings.Remediation: Verify manually: (1) credentials printed and stored in sealed envelope in physical safe, (2) account excluded from MFA CA via named location, (3) sign-in alert configured in Entra ID or Azure Monitor, (4) tested quarterly from known IP.

Findings.Title: Anti-phishing policy with impersonation protection
Findings.Detail: Anti-phishing policy with impersonation protection exists but has no active rule applying it to recipients.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy 'Strict Preset Security Policy1757690553683' configured but no enabled rule targets recipients
Findings.RequiredValue: Policy + active rule covering all accepted domains or all recipients
Findings.Remediation: Create an anti-phishing rule: New-AntiPhishRule -AntiPhishPolicy [name] -RecipientDomainIs [domain] -Priority 0. Or use the Security portal.

Findings.Title: Safe Attachments policy with Block action
Findings.Detail: Safe Attachments Block policy active and applied to recipients.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy: 'Safe Attachments' | Action: Block | Enabled: True
Findings.RequiredValue: Enabled Safe Attachments policy with Action=Block and active rule

Findings.Title: Safe Links policy enabled tenant-wide
Findings.Detail: Safe Links enabled but AllowClickThrough=True — users can bypass Safe Links warnings and reach malicious URLs.
Findings.CurrentValue: Policy: 'Protect Safe Links' | ScanUrls: True | ClickThrough: ALLOWED (gap) | InternalSenders: enabled | Teams: enabled
Findings.RequiredValue: ScanUrls=True, AllowClickThrough=False, EnableForInternalSenders=True, applied to all recipients
Findings.Remediation: Set AllowClickThrough=$false on the Safe Links policy to prevent users bypassing URL block warnings.

Findings.Title: SharePoint tenant external sharing restricted
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Default sharing link type set to internal or specific people
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Anyone link expiration enforced (30 days or less)
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: External user resharing of content disabled
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Unmanaged device access policy limits or blocks SharePoint access
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: OneDrive default sharing link scoped to organisation
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Guest user resharing of items they do not own disabled
Findings.Detail: SharePoint tenant settings not accessible. Verify SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is granted.

Findings.Title: Teams external access (federation) restricted
Findings.Detail: Teams federation is enabled with all external organisations. Any Teams user globally can contact your users and initiate chats or calls.
Findings.RequiredValue: Disabled, or AllowedDomains list with specific trusted organisations only
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Users > External access > Set to Off or configure specific allowed domains.

Findings.Title: Teams consumer (personal account) federation disabled
Findings.Detail: Teams users can communicate with Teams personal (consumer) accounts. This allows unmanaged, unsecured personal accounts to interact with internal users and potentially exfiltrate data.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Users > External access > Disable "Allow users in my organization to communicate with Teams users whose accounts are not managed by an organization".

Findings.Title: Anonymous meeting join disabled
Findings.Detail: Unauthenticated anonymous users can join Teams meetings. Anyone with the meeting link can attend without signing in, with no audit trail.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Disable "Anonymous users can join a meeting".

Findings.Title: Meeting lobby enabled for external and anonymous users
Meeting presenter role restricted to organiser or co-organisers
Findings.Detail: Anyone in a meeting can present by default. External attendees can share screens and content without restriction.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Who can present > Set to "Organizers and co-organizers" or "Specific people".

Findings.Title: PSTN (dial-in) users bypass lobby disabled
Third-party cloud storage apps disabled in Teams
Findings.Detail: Third-party cloud storage enabled in Teams: GoogleDrive, Box, DropBox, ShareFile. Users can share files from unmanaged external storage, bypassing DLP and retention policies.
Findings.CurrentValue: Enabled storage apps: GoogleDrive, Box, DropBox, ShareFile
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Teams apps > Setup policies > Disable Box, Dropbox, Google Drive, ShareFile integrations.

Findings.Title: Email integration into Teams channels disabled
Findings.Detail: Teams channels accept email via a channel email address. Anyone who discovers or is given a channel email address can post content directly into Teams, bypassing message controls.
Findings.Remediation: Teams admin center > Teams settings > Email integration > Uncheck "Allow users to send emails to a channel email address".

Findings.Title: Unified audit log enabled
Findings.Detail: The unified audit log is disabled. All M365 user and admin activity is not being recorded. Incident response and forensics are impossible without this data.
Findings.Remediation: Purview compliance portal > Audit > Turn on auditing. Or: Set-AdminAuditLogConfig -UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled $true

Findings.Title: Audit log retention configured (90 days minimum)
Findings.Detail: Audit log retains 90 days — meets minimum. Recommend extending to 365 days (requires Microsoft 365 E5 or Audit Premium add-on).
Findings.Remediation: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Audit (Premium) add-on required for 1-year retention. Enable via Purview compliance portal > Audit > Audit retention policies.

Findings.Title: DLP policy covers Exchange email
Findings.CurrentValue: 13 DLP policy(ies) cover Exchange: Default Office 365 DLP policy, RiskSpotLighting_EndPoint_2025-10-15 00:18:36.372467, Adaptive Protection policy for Teams and Exchange DLP
Findings.RequiredValue: At least one enabled DLP policy covering Exchange

Findings.Title: DLP policy covers SharePoint and OneDrive
Findings.RequiredValue: At least one DLP policy covering SharePoint/OneDrive

Findings.Title: DLP policy covers Microsoft Teams
Findings.RequiredValue: At least one DLP policy covering Teams

Findings.Title: Retention policy for Exchange email configured
Findings.RequiredValue: At least one retention policy covering Exchange email

Findings.Title: Retention policy for SharePoint and OneDrive configured
Findings.RequiredValue: At least one retention policy covering SharePoint/OneDrive

Findings.Title: Sensitivity labels published to users
Findings.Detail: 5 sensitivity label(s) exist but none are published to users. Labels exist in the system but users cannot apply them to documents.
Findings.RequiredValue: Sensitivity labels published to all or target users via label policy
Findings.Remediation: Purview compliance portal > Information protection > Label policies > Publish labels to users and groups.

Findings.Title: Intune MDM authority configured and devices enrolled
Findings.Detail: Intune is licensed and the API is accessible, but no managed devices found. Endpoint compliance and management policies cannot be enforced until devices are enrolled.
Findings.RequiredValue: Devices enrolled and MDM authority active
Findings.Remediation: Enroll Windows devices via Group Policy, Autopilot, or manual enrollment. Enroll mobile devices via Company Portal app.

Findings.Title: Windows device compliance policy requires BitLocker encryption
Findings.RequiredValue: Windows compliance policy with bitLockerEnabled = true

Findings.Title: Windows device compliance policy requires antivirus and firewall
Findings.Detail: No Windows compliance policy requires antivirus or firewall. Endpoints can be enrolled as "compliant" with no AV or firewall active.
Findings.RequiredValue: Antivirus = Required, Firewall = Required in Windows compliance policy
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Compliance policies > [Windows policy] > System security: Set Firewall = Required, Antivirus = Required, Real-time protection = Required.

Findings.Title: iOS App Protection (MAM) policy configured
Findings.Detail: No iOS App Protection policy configured. Corporate data accessed via Outlook, Teams, or OneDrive on personal iOS devices has no copy/paste, screenshot, or backup restrictions. If a device is lost or an employee leaves, data cannot be remotely wiped.
Findings.RequiredValue: iOS App Protection policy covering Outlook, Teams, OneDrive at minimum
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Apps > App protection policies > Create policy (iOS/iPadOS). Target Outlook, Teams, OneDrive. Set PIN, copy/paste restrictions, and remote wipe capability.

Findings.Title: Android App Protection (MAM) policy configured
Findings.Detail: No Android App Protection policy configured. Corporate data on Android personal devices has no controls. Data can be copied to personal apps, screenshots can be taken, and data cannot be remotely wiped.
Findings.RequiredValue: Android App Protection policy covering Outlook, Teams, OneDrive at minimum
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Apps > App protection policies > Create policy (Android). Target Outlook, Teams, OneDrive. Set PIN, copy/paste, screenshot restrictions.

Findings.Title: Windows Hello for Business policy configured in Intune
Findings.Detail: No Windows Hello for Business policy found. Windows sign-in defaults to password only on enrolled devices. WHfB provides phishing-resistant biometric/PIN authentication at the device level.
Findings.RequiredValue: Windows Hello for Business enrollment policy enabled in Intune
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Enrollment > Windows Hello for Business. Enable and require PIN with biometric option. Or create Identity Protection profile under Device configuration.

Findings.Title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint connector enabled in Intune
Findings.Detail: No Microsoft Defender for Endpoint connector found in Intune. MDE threat risk scores are not integrated with device compliance, meaning compromised devices remain "compliant" in Intune.
Findings.RequiredValue: MDE connector configured and enabled in Intune
Findings.Remediation: Requires Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (P1 or P2) and Intune. Endpoint Manager > Endpoint security > Microsoft Defender for Endpoint > Open MDE portal and enable Intune connection.

Findings.Title: Enrollment restrictions configured beyond default allow-all policy
Findings.Detail: No custom enrollment restrictions found. The default policy allows all device types (Android, iOS, Windows, macOS) to enroll without restriction. Personal/unmanaged devices can enroll without controls.
Findings.CurrentValue: Default enrollment restrictions only (allow all platforms)
Findings.RequiredValue: Enrollment restrictions limiting allowed platforms and requiring corporate ownership
Findings.Remediation: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Create restriction. Block platforms not used in your org. Consider requiring corporate-owned device type.

Findings.Title: Default environment DLP policy configured
Findings.Detail: Power Platform DLP data not available. Install Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell for full DLP assessment: Install-Module Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell

Findings.Title: Power Platform tenant isolation enabled
Findings.Detail: Tenant isolation data not available via Graph beta API. Verify Power Platform is provisioned in this tenant.

Findings.Title: High-risk connectors blocked in Power Platform DLP policies
Findings.Detail: Power Platform DLP data not available. Install Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell for DLP connector assessment.

Findings.Title: Power Platform admin role assignments minimal
Findings.CurrentValue: No dedicated Power Platform admin role assignments (Global Admins have access implicitly)
Findings.RequiredValue: Minimal Power Platform Administrator role assignments

Findings.Title: Non-default Power Platform environments covered by DLP policy
Findings.Detail: Power Platform DLP data not available. Install Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell.
